signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ Decision
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −25,757 (specs heavily short with long% at 19.5%) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for the July 8 meeting. If the RBNZ delivers a 25bp hike to 2.50% or signals a hawkish path, short-squeeze risk is elevated. The crowded speculative short unwind targets a recovery toward 0.5800, while a hold or dovish shift risks capitulation toward 0.5600. NZD/USD is currently trading at 0.5678.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Risk + Carry + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY is holding at 162.09, well above the 160.00 level that previously triggered verbal intervention warnings. While the +266bp SOFR-TONA carry advantage structurally supports the pair, escalating Middle East tensions and the US revocation of Iran's crude oil waiver introduce sharp intervention risks. The options market is actively hedging downside protection, with the 1M 25d RR showing a strong put skew of -1.52, diverging from the crowded COT LF net short of -137,828. Watch for sudden BoJ or MoF verbal warnings to trigger a rapid unwind toward 159.50.

signals.title: AUD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry Divergence
signals.text: AUD/CHF triggers a G10 bilateral theme warning with a 0.810% leader-to-laggard spread. AUD is the strongest G10 currency this week (1W average +0.403% across 4 pairs) while CHF is the weakest (1W average -0.408% across 6 pairs). The divergence is fundamentally driven by a massive +435bp carry premium favoring AUD (RBA 4.35% vs SNB 0.0%), which dominates price action in the current normal volatility regime (VIX at 16.1). Watch for any escalation in global risk-off sentiment to rapidly compress this carry spread and bid CHF.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Risk Premium Extreme
signals.text: AUD/USD options are pricing an extreme volatility risk premium, with 1M implied volatility at 27.8% vs a 30-day realized volatility of 6.9%, resulting in a +20.9% VRP. This massive premium indicates intense institutional demand for downside hedging despite neutral speculative positioning (COT LF net long +21,597, 62.1% long) and a flat retail sentiment split (50% long / 50% short). The options market is pricing in significant tail risk ahead of the next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on August 11. AUD/USD is trading at 0.6928.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Cross-Asset Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/CAD vs WTI Crude Oil 252-day correlation has broken down to -0.881, representing a -2.03σ deviation from its historical norm of +0.010. While WTI crude oil surged +2.73% to $72.4 on Middle East supply disruptions, USD/CAD remained virtually flat at 1.4203. This decoupling indicates that the +133bp SOFR-CORRA carry advantage and safe-haven USD demand amid stock weakness are completely overriding the traditional Canadian oil-export link. Watch the next BoC rate decision on July 15 to see if monetary policy divergence reinforces this break.
